title: Psalms 47
subtitle: God Has Ascended with a Shout

sections.header: Verses 1–5 — “Clap Your Hands, All You Nations”

heb.paragraph: "God has ascended (ʿālâ) amid shouts of joy, the LORD amid the sounding of trumpets" (v.5). An enthronement psalm. God ascends to his throne with shouting and trumpets. The church traditionally reads this for Christ’s ascension (Acts 1:9–11). The nations are invited: clap your hands, all peoples (v.1). God is king over all the earth (v.7).

notes.note: "Clap your hands, all you nations; shout to God with cries of joy. For the LORD Most High is awesome, the great King over all the earth." Universal summons. Not Israel alone but all nations.
"God has ascended amid shouts of joy." The ascension image. Whether describing the ark’s procession or a coronation, the church hears Christ ascending to the Father’s right hand.

heb.gloss: he reigns / he has become king
heb.paragraph: "God reigns (mālak̅) over the nations; God is seated on his holy throne" (v.8). The enthronement declaration. "The nobles of the nations assemble as the people of the God of Abraham" (v.9). The Gentiles join Abraham’s family. The psalm envisions universal worship under one King — the missionary vision of the OT.

notes.note: "God is the King of all the earth; sing to him a psalm of praise. God reigns over the nations." The universal reign: not just Israel’s God but the world’s King.
"The nobles of the nations assemble as the people of the God of Abraham." Gentile inclusion in Abraham’s covenant. This is the Abrahamic promise (Gen 12:3) enacted in worship.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: "God has ascended amid shouts of joy…

ppl.name: Abraham
ppl.text: "The nobles of the nations assemble as the people of the God of Abraham…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVGod has ascended amid shouts of joy, the Lord amid the sounding of trumpets.ESVGod has gone up with a shout, the LORD with the sound of a trumpet.NIVFor the Lord Most High is awesome, the great King over all the earth.ESVFor the LORD, the Most High, is to be feared, a great king over all the earth.

rec.text: ""Clap your hands, all you nations; shout to God with cries of joy. For the LORD Most High is awesome, the great King over all the earth." Universal summons. Not Israel alone but all nations."

rows.label: Universal Reign — 47:6–9
rows.text: "God reigns over the nations." Nobles assemble as Abraham’s people.

hebtext.note: "God has ascended (ʿālâ) amid shouts of joy, the LORD amid the sounding of trumpets" (v.5). An enthronement psalm. God ascends to his throne with shouting and trumpets. The church traditionally reads this for Christ’s ascension (Acts 1:9–11). The nations are invited: clap your hands, all peoples (v.1). God is king over all the earth (v.7).

hebtext.note: "God reigns (mālak̅) over the nations; God is seated on his holy throne" (v.8). The enthronement declaration. "The nobles of the nations assemble as the people of the God of Abraham" (v.9). The Gentiles join Abraham’s family. themes.note: Sovereignty at 10: God reigns over all nations. Worship at 9: universal praise. Mission at 8: Gentiles join Abraham’s family. Prophecy: ascension points to Christ.
